title: What to Know If Your Flight Is Canceled
summary: Airlines around the world are canceling flights because the war in the Middle East is making jet fuel more expensive. This is happening as summer begins, when many people want to travel. If your flight is canceled, you might have time to make new plans because the airlines cancel early. You should check the airline's app or website to book a new flight. For U.S. airlines, this is the fastest way. For other airlines, you may need to call or go to the airport. The article helps travelers understand what to do when flights are canceled.

keywords.explanation: a company that flies planes to take people to different places
the special fuel that planes need to fly
to book a new flight after the old one is canceled

questions.question: What should you do first if your flight is canceled?
options: Check the airline's app or website
Wait for a call from the airline
questions.correct_answer: Check the airline's app or website

questions.question: Which airlines have better digital tools for rebooking?
What is jet fuel used for?
Who can help travelers get compensation for flight disruptions?

background_read: Airlines fly planes to take people to different cities or countries. Sometimes flights are canceled for many reasons, like bad weather or mechanical problems. When a flight is canceled, passengers need to find another way to travel. This article talks about cancellations because jet fuel is expensive.
Article_Structure: WHO: Travelers and airlines around the world.
WHAT: Airlines are canceling flights because jet fuel costs more.
WHERE: Worldwide, but especially in regions affected by the Middle East war.
WHY: The war in the Middle East makes jet fuel more expensive, so airlines cancel flights to save money.

perspectives.description: Airlines cancel flights to save money on fuel.
Travelers may have to change their plans and find new flights.
